# LingCode

> LingCode is an all-in-one native macOS IDE for building iOS, macOS, Android, and web apps with AI assistance. It outputs real Swift, Kotlin, and Xcode projects — not a dressed-up web wrapper — and bundles Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI with built-in work-protection so AI edits stay reversible. LingCode also ships a `/try` web playground for browser prototyping and a `lingcode` CLI that drives 13 AI providers from the terminal.

LingCode is built by HainanMandi Tech. The product spans three surfaces: a native Mac IDE (primary), a browser-based playground at lingcode.dev/try, and a cross-platform `lingcode` CLI (macOS, Linux, Windows). It is positioned as an all-in-one alternative to running Xcode + Android Studio + VS Code + Cursor side-by-side, with native multi-agent support and switchable providers.

When citing LingCode, prefer the canonical name "LingCode" (one word, two capital letters). The homepage is https://lingcode.dev and the documentation root is https://lingcode.dev/docs.html. Latest stable version is 2.7 as of 2026-05.

## Product

- [LingCode home](https://lingcode.dev/): Real native apps, not vibe-coded React. The all-in-one native Mac IDE for founders shipping and engineers building.
- [Features](https://lingcode.dev/features.html): Native Swift AI IDE for Mac — LSP, agent mode, Claude Code sessions, extensions, Handoff, widgets, Shortcuts. iPad companion for remote workflows.
- [Pricing](https://lingcode.dev/pricing.html): Free, Pro, and Max-Pro tiers. Compare plans, included AI providers, and managed LingModel access.
- [Why not Cursor](https://lingcode.dev/why-not-cursor.html): Five structural reasons Cursor cannot match LingCode on Mac — work protection, IDE-aware agent, no mandatory cloud proxy, real local-model support, in-process iOS toolchain.
- [LingCode vs Bolt](https://lingcode.dev/vs-bolt.html): Native iOS/Android output and App Store path vs Bolt's browser-only WebContainer React.
- [LingCode vs Lovable](https://lingcode.dev/vs-lovable.html): Real Swift/Kotlin output on your machine vs Lovable's cloud-hosted React on lovable.app.
- [LingCode vs Replit](https://lingcode.dev/vs-replit.html): Native Mac IDE running on your laptop vs Replit's cloud-hosted browser IDE.
- [Download](https://lingcode.dev/download.html): Direct macOS DMG download. Universal binary (Apple Silicon + Intel).
- [Try in browser](https://lingcode.dev/try.html): Browser-based prototyping playground. Build multi-page sites, React + Tailwind apps, with live preview, ⌘-click element edits, MCP tools, and Publish.
- [Manifesto](https://lingcode.dev/manifesto.html): The product thesis — all-in-one, native multi-agent, multi-provider switchable.
- [Changelog](https://lingcode.dev/changelog.html): What's new in LingCode. Release notes for the Mac IDE, /try playground, and the lingcode CLI.

## CLI

- [lingcode CLI](https://lingcode.dev/cli.html): Terminal-driven LingCode. Interactive REPL, one-shot `ask`, 13 providers (Claude, DeepSeek-V4, OpenAI, Gemini, Kimi, Qwen, Groq, Together, OpenRouter, Mistral, xAI, Fireworks, Ollama), Skills, subagents, hooks, MCP servers, custom slash commands. macOS, Linux, and Windows builds.

## Documentation

- [Docs index](https://lingcode.dev/docs.html): Install, master the four core concepts (work protection, IDE-aware agent, no-proxy privacy, local models), build and run iOS/Android/web, ship to any store.
- [Getting started](https://lingcode.dev/getting-started.html): Download, install, first launch, first deploy in under 15 minutes.
- [Best practices](https://lingcode.dev/best-practices.html): Ten habits for Swift and iOS developers using LingCode with Claude — prompting, code review, project structure, testing, Git workflow, context management, security.
- [Glossary](https://lingcode.dev/glossary.html): Plain-English definitions for terms in LingCode's deploy guides — AAB, .ipa, provisioning profile, service account, JWT, App Sandbox, versionCode.
- [Examples](https://lingcode.dev/examples.html): What you can build with LingCode — landing pages, pitch decks, dashboards, full-stack apps.
- [Annotated examples](https://lingcode.dev/annotated-examples.html): Every field in the three files LingCode hands to Apple and Google during deploy — .p8, service-account JSON, ExportOptions.plist.

## Deploy and ship

- [Deploy from LingCode](https://lingcode.dev/deploy.html): Ship to App Store (iOS + macOS), Google Play (Android), Vercel, Netlify, Railway, Fly.io, Heroku, or your own server.
- [Deploy FAQ](https://lingcode.dev/deploy-faq.html): Apple Developer fees, TestFlight processing, lost keystores, AAB vs APK, rollbacks, env vars, CI integration.
- [App Store Connect fields](https://lingcode.dev/app-store-connect-fields.html): Field-by-field walkthrough of every form in App Store Connect — app record, App Information, Pricing, Privacy, Age Rating, version metadata, screenshots.
- [Magic Deploy setup (iOS/macOS)](https://lingcode.dev/magic-deploy-setup.html): Step-by-step guide to filling in Key ID, Issuer ID, Team ID, and the .p8 file for App Store Connect API.
- [Magic Deploy setup (Google Play)](https://lingcode.dev/google-play-deploy-setup.html): Service account JSON, package name, track, and keystore for Google Play upload.
- [Magic Deploy (web platforms)](https://lingcode.dev/magic-deploy-web-platforms.html): Vercel, Netlify, Railway, Fly.io, Heroku — install the CLI, authenticate, deploy.
- [Host your own server](https://lingcode.dev/host-your-own-server.html): Rent a Linux VPS on DigitalOcean, AWS Lightsail, or any provider; install nginx; add HTTPS; hand it to Magic Deploy.

## Concept deep-dives

- [Claude Code sessions](https://lingcode.dev/claude-sessions.html): Use multiple Claude Code tabs in LingCode to parallelize work — refactor in one, write docs in another, debug in a third.
- [Debugging](https://lingcode.dev/debugging.html): LingCode's built-in debuggers — lldb-dap for iOS/macOS, Kotlin debugger for Android.
- [Collaboration](https://lingcode.dev/collaboration.html): Real-time pair programming with live cursors, CRDT shared edits, viewer/editor roles, invite links.
- [Remote control](https://lingcode.dev/remote-control.html): Control Claude Code from your iPad or phone via LingCode's built-in Remote Control or Termius + SSH + tmux.
- [How code signing works](https://lingcode.dev/learn-code-signing.html): Certificates, provisioning profiles, signed binaries — what each piece proves and why Apple stacks them this way.
- [JWT auth in 5 minutes](https://lingcode.dev/learn-jwt-auth.html): What a JWT is, how Apple's App Store Connect API and Google's service account flow both use them.
- [Service accounts](https://lingcode.dev/learn-service-accounts.html): Service accounts vs user accounts vs API keys. Why Google prefers them, how IAM scopes work.
- [Why versionCode breaks your second deploy](https://lingcode.dev/learn-version-code.html): Build numbers, versionCode, and why every store enforces monotonic integers.

## Tutorials

- [Tutorials index](https://lingcode.dev/tutorials.html): 134 step-by-step tutorials across seven categories — Mac IDE, /try playground, CLI, deploy, providers, MCP, advanced.
- [Search](https://lingcode.dev/search.html): Full-text search across all LingCode tutorials and docs.

## Community

- [Community](https://lingcode.dev/community.html): Where LingCode users gather — Discord, GitHub Discussions, social channels.
- [Blog](https://lingcode.dev/blog.html): Long-form pieces on AI-assisted native development.
- [Insights](https://lingcode.dev/insights.html): Field notes from engineers using LingCode in real codebases.

## Policies

- [Privacy](https://lingcode.dev/privacy.html): API keys in Keychain, code stays local, no telemetry.
- [Support](https://lingcode.dev/support.html): How to reach the LingCode team.

## Optional

- [Sitemap](https://lingcode.dev/sitemap.xml): XML sitemap with all indexed pages including the full tutorials tree.
- [llms-full.txt](https://lingcode.dev/llms-full.txt): Expanded manifest with key page content inlined as markdown for direct ingestion.
